name: scratch
label: scratch
whenToUse: Statically-linked Go (CGO_ENABLED=0) or Rust binaries. Zero attack surface required. No CVEs possible — no packages.
whenNot: Any binary that requires libc, shell, dynamic linking, or CA certificates (use Distroless static instead).
primaryTradeoff: Zero attack surface, zero image size vs zero tooling — no shell, no certs, no debug tools. Distroless static adds ~2MB but includes CA certs.

name: Alpine Linux 3.21
label: Alpine Linux 3.21
whenToUse: Smallest possible image where no musl-incompatible binary is in the stack. Go (CGO disabled), Python (pure-Python packages), static Rust.
whenNot: Any binary compiled against glibc — Python native extensions (cryptography, lxml, numpy), Node.js native modules (bcrypt, sharp), or cgo-enabled Go.
primaryTradeoff: Smallest image (~7MB vs Debian-slim ~75MB) vs musl libc breaks ~15% of precompiled binaries without rebuild.

name: UBI-micro 9
label: UBI-micro 9
whenToUse: FIPS required. Red Hat / OpenShift environment. No runtime package installation needed — all packages layered at build time in Dockerfile.
whenNot: Need to install packages at container runtime. Need a shell in the final image. Non-Red Hat environment.
primaryTradeoff: Smallest RHEL-based FIPS image (~27MB) vs no package manager — must build all layers in the Dockerfile, cannot dnf install at runtime.

name: UBI-minimal 9
label: UBI-minimal 9
whenToUse: FIPS required. Need to install a small number of packages. Red Hat ecosystem.
whenNot: Need full dnf (use UBI-standard). Pure build-time layering is sufficient (use UBI-micro and save 10MB).
primaryTradeoff: FIPS + microdnf package installer vs 10MB larger than UBI-micro. Full dnf is not available — use UBI-standard for that.

name: Distroless (gcr.io)
label: Distroless (gcr.io)
whenToUse: glibc binary compat required. No shell in the final image. Go, Java, Python, Node.js apps where language-specific variant is available.
whenNot: FIPS required (use UBI). Need a shell in the final image. RHEL/OpenShift environment.
primaryTradeoff: glibc compat + no shell vs no FIPS, no official CIS benchmark, Debian lifecycle dependency.

name: Wolfi / Chainguard
label: Wolfi / Chainguard
fromTag: cgr.dev/chainguard/wolfi-base:latestNote: Wolfi's :latest is a rolling release — pin to a date-stamped digest for reproducible builds (e.g. cgr.dev/chainguard/wolfi-base:latest@sha256:...).
whenToUse: SBOM-native required. Supply chain compliance (SLSA Level 3, EO 14028). glibc needed but Alpine-sized image wanted.
whenNot: Free tier only with strict budget. Red Hat / OpenShift ecosystem. DISA STIG required.
primaryTradeoff: SBOM-native + glibc + Alpine-sized image vs newer ecosystem with fewer third-party packages, FIPS requires paid Chainguard subscription.

name: Azure Linux 3 (CBL-Mariner)
label: Azure Linux 3 (CBL-Mariner)
whenToUse: Azure-native workloads. AKS clusters. FIPS on Azure Government. Microsoft enterprise support required.
whenNot: Non-Azure environments. RHEL ecosystem required. DISA STIG officially required.
primaryTradeoff: FIPS + Azure-native + tdnf package manager vs Microsoft-locked, small community, 2-year release cycle.

name: Debian 12 (Bookworm)
label: Debian 12 (Bookworm)
subtitle: stable / slim
whenToUse: Maximum package availability. glibc binary compat for every pre-compiled binary. Base image for Ubuntu, Kali, Mint. No FIPS required.
whenNot: FIPS required. Enterprise vendor support SLA required. RHEL ecosystem expected.
primaryTradeoff: Largest package ecosystem + free vs no FIPS, no vendor support SLA, no DISA STIG.

name: Ubuntu 22.04 LTS
label: Ubuntu 22.04 LTS
subtitle: (Jammy Jellyfish)
whenToUse: Most common dev environment. FIPS needed with Canonical support (Ubuntu Pro — FIPS 140-2 module). DISA STIG required. 22.04 still needed when 24.04 toolchain compatibility is uncertain.
whenNot: RHEL ecosystem required. RPM-native tooling. Budget constraint (FIPS requires Ubuntu Pro paid tier).
primaryTradeoff: Most common OS (lowest developer friction) vs FIPS requires Ubuntu Pro subscription, DISA STIG tooling requires Pro.

name: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS
label: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS
subtitle: (Noble Numbat)
whenToUse: Default GitHub Actions runner (ubuntu-latest since Jan 2025). New projects. Longest support window (2036 with Legacy Support). Latest toolchain (Python 3.12, GCC 13, kernel 6.8). DISA STIG published 2025.
whenNot: FIPS independently certified required — 24.04 FIPS 140-3 module is in validation progress via Ubuntu Pro; use 22.04 if a current FIPS 140-2 certificate number is required by the auditor.
primaryTradeoff: Longest support + default CI runner + DISA STIG vs FIPS 140-3 certification still completing (use 22.04 for FIPS-required environments until 24.04 cert is finalized).

name: Fedora 42
label: Fedora 42
whenToUse: Testing new RHEL features before they land in RHEL. Cutting-edge package versions. Developer workstations.
whenNot: Production container base (13-month EOL). FIPS independently certified required. DISA STIG or CIS required. Long-running servers.
primaryTradeoff: Latest RHEL-bound features + free vs 13-month lifecycle, not production-safe for servers or containers.

name: Amazon Linux 2023
label: Amazon Linux 2023
useContext: Container base · EC2 · Lambda · CI runner
whenToUse: AWS-native workloads. Lambda container images. EC2 with FIPS in GovCloud. AWS manages kernel patches for Nitro hypervisor.
whenNot: Non-AWS environments. RHEL ecosystem migration. OpenShift. On-premises bare-metal.
primaryTradeoff: AWS-optimized kernel + FIPS + free on EC2 vs AWS-locked, packages lag RHEL by one cycle, no DISA STIG officially.

name: VMware Photon OS 5
label: VMware Photon OS 5
whenToUse: VMware vSphere / Tanzu / Broadcom ecosystem. vSphere-native container workloads. FIPS-capable environment on VMware (Photon 5 FIPS validation in progress).
whenNot: FIPS certificate number required by auditor — Photon 5 FIPS validation not yet independently certified (Photon 3.0 had the last certified module). Broadcom licensing uncertainty post-acquisition. No CIS benchmark.
primaryTradeoff: VMware-optimized vs FIPS not independently certified for Photon 5 (Photon 3.0 module held the certificate), Broadcom acquisition, small community.

name: RHEL 9
label: RHEL 9
whenToUse: FedRAMP High, DoD IL4+, financial sector. FIPS + DISA STIG both required. Red Hat subscription available. ISV software with RHEL certification.
whenNot: No Red Hat subscription budget (self-support from $349/year/server, Standard ~$800, Premium ~$1,300). Cost-sensitive non-regulated workload. Non-enterprise use.
primaryTradeoff: Best compliance coverage (FIPS + STIG + CIS all official) vs requires paid Red Hat subscription.

name: CentOS Stream 9
label: CentOS Stream 9
whenToUse: Testing RHEL 9 features pre-release. No subscription cost. Dev/staging environments where pre-release patches are acceptable.
whenNot: Production. FIPS certification required. DISA STIG required. Any regulated environment.
primaryTradeoff: Free + RHEL-compatible package format vs receives changes before RHEL QA, not production-safe.

name: Rocky Linux 9
label: Rocky Linux 9
whenToUse: RHEL-compatible, no subscription cost. Community support acceptable. Non-DoD/non-FedRAMP environments.
whenNot: Independent FIPS certificate required. DISA STIG officially required. Red Hat support SLA required. ISV RHEL certification required.
primaryTradeoff: Free RHEL-compatible vs FIPS not independently certified (uses RHEL crypto code without its own certificate number), community support only.

name: AlmaLinux 9
label: AlmaLinux 9
whenToUse: CentOS 7/8 migration path. RHEL-compatible. CloudLinux commercial backing. Community support acceptable.
whenNot: Independent FIPS certificate required. Red Hat support SLA required. ISV RHEL certification required.
primaryTradeoff: Free + CentOS migration tooling + CloudLinux backing vs same FIPS caveat as Rocky (no independent certificate).

name: Oracle Linux 9
label: Oracle Linux 9
eolSupport: Until RHEL 9 EOL (2032) + Oracle Premier Support
whenToUse: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI). Oracle Database on Linux. FIPS independently certified required. DISA STIG required (OL9 STIG published 2024). Oracle enterprise support required.
whenNot: Non-Oracle environments. AWS/Azure native. No Oracle licensing budget. Red Hat ISV certification required (Oracle clone does not carry Red Hat cert).
primaryTradeoff: Independently FIPS-certified + official DISA STIG + Oracle support vs Oracle licensing costs for Premier Support, OCI-centric kernel optimizations.

name: Flatcar Container Linux
label: Flatcar Container Linux
whenToUse: Kubernetes node OS. Immutable OS required. Atomic OS updates with auto-rollback. Azure / AKS workloads. SSH still available (vs Talos).
whenNot: Non-Kubernetes workloads. Need to install host-level packages. FIPS required. DISA STIG required.
primaryTradeoff: Immutable + auto-update + SSH available vs no FIPS, no DISA STIG, Microsoft-controlled release cadence.

name: Talos Linux 1.10
label: Talos Linux 1.10
whenToUse: Kubernetes-only clusters. Maximum attack surface reduction — no SSH, no shell, no unnecessary processes. GitOps-driven cluster management via talosctl API.
whenNot: Any non-Kubernetes workload. Need SSH access to nodes. Need custom kernel modules. FIPS certificate independently required. DISA STIG required.
primaryTradeoff: Smallest attack surface of any K8s node OS (no shell, no SSH) vs absolutely no host-level interactive access, ~1-year release lifecycle.

name: Bottlerocket 1.x
label: Bottlerocket 1.x
whenToUse: EKS on AWS. FIPS in GovCloud. Auto-patching required. Minimal attack surface on K8s nodes with AWS management integration (SSM).
whenNot: Non-EKS Kubernetes. On-premises. DISA STIG officially required. Non-AWS environments.
primaryTradeoff: FIPS + immutable + AWS-managed patching vs AWS-locked, no DISA STIG, no SSH (admin container required for debug).

name: Google COS
label: Google COS
minImage: ~2.5 GB (GKE node OS — not a Dockerfile FROM image)
whenToUse: GKE node OS. Automatic security patching required. No node-level customization needed.
whenNot: Non-GKE Kubernetes. Need custom kernel modules. Need host-level packages. DISA STIG required.
primaryTradeoff: Auto-patched + container-optimized kernel vs immutable (cannot apt-get on the node), 1-year release cycle, GKE-locked.
